title: 3 days after my dad's funeral I found a second will, and the name on it wasn't anyone in our family
script: Three days after we buried my dad, I found a second will hidden in his desk drawer that left our family lake house to a woman named Talia Winslow, someone I had never heard of in thirty-four years of being his daughter. My brother Josh said to burn it and pretend we never saw it, but I couldn't stop staring at the date, it was signed two years before dad died, right after our mom's funeral. I went through his old phone bill and found one number he had called every single Sunday night for six years, always for exactly twenty minutes, and I dialed it with my hands shaking. A girl's voice answered, she sounded maybe fifteen, and she asked if I was calling about her dad. Then she said his name. It was spelled exactly the way my father used to sign my birthday cards.
